name: Barbecued Beef Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the steak to be 1/2 to 3/4-inch thick and weigh approximately 2 to 2 1/2 lbs.
2. Combine catsup, chili sauce, brown sugar, wine vinegar, steak sauce, cumin, and chili powder in a small saucepan; cook slowly for 20 minutes.
3. Remove the bone from the round steak and sprinkle with tenderizer, salt, and pepper. Pound with a mallet.
4. Brush the top of the steak with half cup barbecue sauce.
5. Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese, corn, green pepper, and sliced ripe olives on the steak.
6. Begin with the long side and roll the steak tightly. Tie with string.
7. Dredge the steak roll in flour and lightly brown in cooking oil in an electric frying pan.
8. Pour remaining barbecue sauce and water over the steak. Cover tightly and cook slowly for 1 1/2 hours or until meat is tender.
9. During the last 5 minutes of cooking, remove strings and sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese and sliced ripe olives on top of the meat.
10. Place the steak roll on a warm serving platter. Garnish with tomato roses.
11. Place green pepper slices around the tomatoes for a leaf effect.
12. Serve remaining barbecue sauce with the beef roll.
Note: Tomato Roses - Using 3 tomatoes, pare each with a very sharp knife, starting at the rounded top and continuing in a circular manner around the tomato to obtain a strip of peel approximately 10 inches long and 1 inch wide (pare off only the skin). Wrap one end of one strip around the tip of your index finger. Continue wrapping, forming the petals of a rose. Gently lift the tomato rose from your finger to the serving platter. Repeat for other roses.
